creator: omar@megamigos.pet
text:
I am running:
# uname -a
FreeBSD lab 14.1-RELEASE-p5 FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE-p5 GENERIC amd64

When I attempt to install websocketd in this new system, I get the following:

# pkg install websocketd
The package management tool is not yet installed on your system.
Do you want to fetch and install it now? [y/N]: y
Bootstrapping pkg from pkg+https://pkg.FreeBSD.org/FreeBSD:14:amd64/quarterly, please wait...
Verifying signature with trusted certificate pkg.freebsd.org.2013102301... done
[lab] Installing pkg-1.21.3...
[lab] Extracting pkg-1.21.3: 100%
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
[lab] Fetching data.pkg: 100%    7 MiB 621.1kB/s    00:12
Processing entries: 100%
FreeBSD repository update completed. 35525 packages processed.
All repositories are up to date.
Updating database digests format: 100%
pkg: No packages available to install matching 'websocketd' have been found in the repositories

In the past, I was able to install websocketd without issues. As an example, in a different machine I get:

# ls -l /usr/local/bin/websocketd
-r-xr-xr-x  1 root wheel 5705600 Jan  1  2024 /usr/local/bin/websocketd
